brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer of 3000 with valid N×L derivation; goes three levels deep — per-instance mechanics, general formula, and fix tradeoffs; addresses the misleading framing of the 900 req/min example but does not explicitly name cognitive biases or course-correct mid-analysis; uses 2500 req/min as a boundary condition test but stops short of a full counterfactual derivation; acknowledges the even-distribution assumption breaks under dynamic scaling but presents most conclusions without stated confidence levels; mentions two distinct fix approaches (shared counter vs. L/N per instance) with a tradeoff note, though does not evaluate deeper alternatives like sliding-window algorithms; every conclusion traces directly to an explicit formula with enough detail that a third party could reproduce the full chain.
